WANC × K26 — Nisan 2026
We Are Not Creative × Kollektiv 26 — Claude Design Handoff v1.0
KOLLEKTIV 26
Website Design System — Single Source Document
Type
Contemporary cultural platform
Stack
Next.js + Tailwind + Sanity CMS
Languages
TR + EN — URL based /tr/ prefix
Version
Handoff v1.0 — April 2026

This document is the single source of truth for Claude Design. It contains brand foundation, all locked design decisions, visual identity system, site architecture, page-by-page layout direction with layout references from the mock-up PDF, and all approved copy in EN + TR. Mock-up PDF layouts are referenced by page number throughout this document. Do not override decisions marked as LOCKED.

01
Brand Foundation
Context for all decisions

What Kollektiv 26 Is

Kollektiv 26 operates as a concierge platform shaped by curation, context and cultural perspective. Rather than separating travel, culture, music and experience into service categories, it approaches them as a continuous field defined by selection, atmosphere and intention. It moves between destinations, disciplines and moments — from the formation of an idea to the experience it becomes. Positioned beyond conventional concierge and hospitality frameworks, Kollektiv 26 is built as a contemporary cultural system.

What It Is Not

×
Not a traditional concierge brand
×
Not a festival-first identity
×
Not a hospitality cliché
×
Not a tech-driven service platform

Active Modes

ACTIVE — FAZ 1
Tourism & Concierge
Most developed mode. Private travel, curated stays, lifestyle coordination. Aegean base.
DEVELOPING — FAZ 2
Entertainment
Music programming, private gatherings, cultural activations. Still forming, forming with intention.
FUTURE — FAZ 3
Innovation
Concept development, new brand creation. Visible on site, not yet open. Coming soon page only.

Brand Voice — Rules

HOW WE WRITE
→Calm and confident. We don't raise our voice.
→Editorial. One sentence can carry a world.
→Specific. "Sapphire coast" not "luxury destination."
→Occasionally poetic but never vague.
→Human. Knowledgeable friend, not corporate voice.
HOW WE DON'T WRITE
×No hype: "unforgettable," "world-class," "once-in-a-lifetime"
×No lists. We write sentences.
×No imperatives: "Discover now," "Book today"
×No jargon: "all-inclusive," "seamless," "turnkey"
×No over-explanation. Brevity is intelligence.

Words We Use / Avoid

Use

AccessCuratedDeliberateRareBetweenTasteCollectiveCommissionMemberAtmosphereContextEditorialCulturalConcept-drivenContemporary

Avoid

LuxuryPremiumExclusiveVIPSeamlessUnforgettableUniqueBespokeWorld-classTailor-madeMeaningful by designUnforgettable by nature
02
Locked Decisions
Do not override

Site Architecture — LOCKED

Model B — Final Home = gateway / entry point
Tourism & Concierge = separate landing + sub-pages
Entertainment = separate landing + sub-pages
Innovation = visible but locked / coming soon
About = global
Contact = global

Navbar — LOCKED

Navbar Structure LEFT: Logo (K26 mark + KOLLEKTIV 26) + active mode line below logo
CENTRE: Home / About / Contact
RIGHT: Enquire (arrow link) + menu trigger (hamburger)

Active mode line examples:
— Home: nothing or neutral
— Tourism active: "Tourism & Concierge" (serif, small)
— Entertainment active: "Entertainment" (sans, small, uppercase)
— Innovation active: "Innovation / Coming Soon"

Navbar behaviour: frosted glass / blur-tint overlay. NOT heavy glassmorphism.
Premium editorial overlay — subtle background blur, slight warm tint.
Logo always white on dark navbar. Black on light navbar when scrolled.

Mode Panel — LOCKED

Mode Panel Behaviour Triggered by menu icon (hamburger) top right.
Shows mode switcher at top (Tourism / Entertainment / Innovation tabs).
Below: only active mode's sub-pages. NOT a sitemap dump.

Tourism selected → Overview / Destinations / Itineraries / Concierge / Enquire
Entertainment selected → Overview / Focus / Coming Soon
Innovation selected → Coming Soon only

Panel feels like a navigational teaser. Clean, minimal, editorial.
Reference: Mock-up PDF pages 3, 7, 8 for panel layout.

Home Mode Circles — LOCKED

Places / Moments / Futures Three floating circles over atmospheric hero image.
Places → Tourism & Concierge
Moments → Entertainment
Futures → Innovation (disabled / dimmed)

Circles: glassmorphic, frosted, slightly blurred background.
Each has a small yellow dot indicator.
Hover: subtle scale + blur shift. Click: navigates to mode.
Reference: Mock-up PDF pages 1, 2, 3, 4 for circle positioning.

Tonal Differentiation — LOCKED

HOME
Neutral / gateway. Warm off-white background. Neither Tourism nor Entertainment. Bridge.
TOURISM
Open tones. Serif visible. Editorial, premium, calm. Warm off-white / stone / cream.
ENTERTAINMENT
Black dominant. Yellow active. Sans only. Energetic, cultural, typographic.
INNOVATION
Jet black. Amber/yellow glow blur. Minimal. Contemporary coming soon.

What to Remove From Mock-ups

Remove — All Instances"THREE WORLDS. ONE CULTURE." — replace with nothing or use "Between worlds."
"Experiences that stay with you." — remove, replace with approved copy
"Meaningful by design. Unforgettable by nature." — remove completely
"WELCOME" navbar label — remove
"Entertainment Amplified" — remove, replace with approved copy
"Built to move people and shape what's next" — remove
"From iconic coastlines to hidden gems..." — remove
"Thoughtfully curated travel experiences, designed around you" — remove
"24/7 support, arranged with discretion" — remove
Orange/amber glow on Innovation — replace with yellow #E8C832 glow

What to Keep From Mock-ups

Keep — Reference ThesePDF p.1: Floating circles system — layout, positioning, glassmorphism level. Perfect.
PDF p.3: Mode panel open state — tab switcher + sub-page list layout.
PDF p.5: Split mode — Tourism left/light, Entertainment right/dark. Use this for homepage scroll section.
PDF p.9: Tourism accordion layout — left text + accordion, right full-bleed image.
PDF p.11: Tourism serif headline "Tourism & Concierge." layout — clean, editorial.
PDF p.15: Entertainment overview — black base, Overview/Focus/Coming Soon cards.
PDF p.19: Innovation coming soon — left-aligned "COMING SOON." yellow dot, amber blur right.
03
Visual Identity
Logo system & symbol

Symbol — K26 Mark

SPINE
Vertical axis
Fixed vertical element. Anchors the mark. Gives strength and stability.
ARCS
Expanding curves
Movement, openness, outward direction. Subtly suggests waves or music.
CORE
Circular centre
Focus, balance, clear recognition point. Holds the system in place.

Design logic: equal stroke weight / hard-cut terminals / modular radial spacing / clear visual hierarchy. Inspired by agora and amphitheatre — not literal, a quiet reference to spaces shaped for gathering.

Signature Lockups

1. PrimarySymbol + KOLLEKTIV 26 (Polymath). Core identity across site headers, presentations, general communication.
2. TourismSymbol + KOLLEKTIV 26 + "Tourism & Concierge" (The Seasons, serif below). Tourism-facing contexts.
3. EntertainmentSymbol + KOLLEKTIV 26 + ENTERTAINMENT (sans-serif, caps below). Entertainment-facing contexts.
4. ReducedSymbol + KOLLEKTIV 26 only (no descriptor). Compact / editorial / established contexts.
Logo SVG file (Varlık_1.svg) is the approved mark. Use this file directly. Do not recreate.
04
Typography System
A structural core with a flexible editorial voice
CORE TYPEFACE
Polymath Display
Structural · Contemporary · Precise · Calm · System-led

Core brand naming, navigation, interface, all headings, all entertainment applications.

Semibold / BoldPrimary brand headlines
Regular / MediumSecondary headings
RegularInterface / support text
LightQuieter emphasis when needed
EDITORIAL TYPEFACE
The Seasons
Editorial · Cultural · Refined · Atmospheric · Expressive

Tourism descriptors, editorial sublines, destination storytelling, selected hero moments where slower atmospheric tone is needed.

RegularEditorial headlines / tourism descriptors
MediumSelective emphasis — tourism layouts only

Usage Modes

CORE BRAND MODE
Polymath leads
Identity, navigation, interface language, general brand communication. All pages use this as base.
TOURISM EDITORIAL MODE
The Seasons may lead
Tourism pages, destination storytelling, curated editorial layouts, slower visual compositions. Serif subtly visible.
ENTERTAINMENT MODE
Polymath only
All entertainment pages. Tighter rhythm. Direct hierarchy. Sans-only descriptor system. No serif.

Hierarchy Principle

Rules — At identity level, Polymath is the primary voice always.
— At application level, The Seasons may lead selected tourism compositions.
— 26 always integrated but optically quieter than KOLLEKTIV.
— Descriptors always secondary within lockups.
— On Entertainment: zero serif usage.
05
Colour System
A restrained palette with tonal expansion

Core Palette — 70%

Black
#0A0A0A
Warm Off-White
#F2EFE8
Off-White
#F8F5EF
Stone
#C8C2B4

Warm Tone — 20%

Supports Tourism, editorial, destination-facing applications.

Dusty Saffron
#C8A96E
Warm Sand
#D4B896
Muted Amber
#B8946A

Signal Tone — 10%

Accent only. Cultural, digital, event-led moments. Yellow dot indicators. CTA arrows.

Sharp Citron
#C8C832
Bright Yellow
#E8C832

Mode-Based Colour Behaviour

HOME
Off-white base. Warm tones. Black type. Yellow accents only on indicators and CTAs.
TOURISM
Off-white / warm stone base. Full-bleed atmospheric images. Minimal yellow usage.
ENTERTAINMENT
Black dominant. White type. Yellow active on event cards, accents, indicators. High contrast.
INNOVATION
Jet black only. Yellow glow blur (#E8C832 not orange). White type. Minimal detail lines.
06
Motion & Interaction
Inspired by Antaeus Travel — deliberate, not decorative

Guiding Principle

Motion is used to reveal, not to impress. Every interaction should feel like the site is breathing — not performing. Reference: Antaeus Travel's animated circles were specifically admired. The goal is subtle interactivity that rewards attention without demanding it.

Approved Motion Patterns

Home — Floating CirclesThe Places / Moments / Futures circles float with a very slow, independent vertical oscillation. Each circle on its own timing (staggered phase). Amplitude: 8–12px. Duration: 6–8 seconds per cycle. CSS-only preferred. On hover: slight scale (1.03) + blur reduction + yellow dot brightens. Reference: Antaeus departure board animation logic — reveals without announcing.
Page Transitions — Mode SwitchWhen switching between Tourism and Entertainment: page background crossfades. Tourism → Entertainment: white fades to black over 400ms. Entertainment → Tourism: black fades to warm white. Typography fades out then in. Navbar mode line updates with fade. No slide animations.
Navbar — Scroll BehaviourOn scroll: navbar background blur increases slightly. Active mode indicator (yellow dot) always visible. On mobile: navbar collapses to logo + menu only. Smooth, no jump.
Tourism — AccordionAccordion items open with a smooth height animation (300ms ease). Icon rotates 45°. Image on right remains static — content slides in below or adjacent. No bounce, no overshoot.
Entertainment — System MarkersSmall waveform / audio level indicators are decorative and static by default. On hover or when Entertainment mode is active: subtle pulse animation. These are system-language details — not interactive, just alive. Avoid making them feel like a music player dashboard.
Innovation — GlowThe yellow blur in the background of the Innovation page has a very slow radial pulse — expands and contracts (scale 0.95 to 1.05) over 8 seconds. Opacity variation: 0.7 to 1.0. Feels like something breathing, waiting.
CTAs — Arrow BehaviourAll arrow CTAs (→): on hover, arrow moves 4px right. Parent element: subtle underline fade in. Duration: 200ms. Consistent across all modes.

Motion — Do Not

AvoidNo parallax scrolling on images — creates nausea, feels dated.
No page loading spinners — fade in instead.
No bounce/spring easing on navigation.
No auto-playing video backgrounds.
No scroll-jacking.
No entrance animations on every single element — only structural reveals on first viewport.
07
Site Architecture
LOCKED — Model B

URL Structure

/ Home Gateway. Neutral. Mode entry via circles + scroll sections. ACTIVE
/tourism Tourism & Concierge Overview + sub-pages: /destinations, /itineraries, /concierge ACTIVE
/entertainment Entertainment Overview + Focus + Coming Soon sections on single page. DEVELOPING
/innovation Innovation Single page. Coming soon only. Visible in nav, not expanded. COMING SOON
/about About Global page. Brand identity, "26" narrative, philosophy, structure. ACTIVE
/contact Contact Global page. Inquiry types + form. Minimal. ACTIVE
/tr/* Turkish versions All pages have /tr/ prefix versions. TR/EN toggle in navbar right area. ACTIVE
08
Navbar System
LOCKED
Navbar Layout — Desktop [K26 MARK] [KOLLEKTIV 26] [Home] [About] [Contact] [Enquire →] [≡]
         [active mode line]

Component States

Default / HomeLogo + no mode line. Centre nav: Home active (yellow underline dot). Enquire + menu right.
Tourism ActiveLogo + "Tourism & Concierge" in The Seasons serif below. Mode label small, italic.
Entertainment ActiveLogo + "Entertainment" in Polymath sans, uppercase, small. Navbar background darkens slightly.
Innovation ActiveLogo + "Innovation / Coming Soon". Navbar on dark background.
Scroll behaviourBackground blur increases. Stays fixed to top. No height change.
MobileLogo left. Menu icon right. No centre nav. Enquire disappears, accessible via menu.

Visual Treatment

Frosted glass effect. Background: rgba(248,245,239,0.85) with backdrop-filter: blur(12px). Border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.06). On dark pages (Entertainment, Innovation): rgba(10,10,10,0.85) with same blur. This is a premium editorial overlay — not trendy glassmorphism. Restrained and functional.

09
Mode Panel
Navigation teaser — not a sitemap
Panel Structure — Reference PDF p.3 Triggered by hamburger (≡) top right.
Panel opens as overlay panel (not full-screen takeover).
Top area: three tabs — [TOURISM & CONCIERGE] [ENTERTAINMENT] [INNOVATION ↗ coming soon]
Below: sub-pages of selected mode only.

Tourism selected: Overview → Destinations → Itineraries → Concierge → Enquire →
Entertainment: Overview → Focus → Coming Soon →
Innovation: Coming Soon →

Panel close: X button top right of panel. Click outside. Escape key.

Panel Visual Treatment

BackgroundWhite / warm off-white with subtle blur. Clean, editorial.
TypographyMode tabs: Polymath caps, small. Sub-pages: Polymath regular, larger. Each with → arrow.
Active mode tabYellow underline indicator. Bold weight.
Innovation tabGreyed / reduced opacity. Lock icon or "coming soon" label. Still clickable → goes to /innovation.
AnimationPanel slides in from right (400ms). Sub-page list fades in after tab selection (200ms).

Home Page — Layout Direction
Gateway — neutral — atmospheric
SECTION 01Hero / EntryFULL VIEWPORT
Layout Reference — PDF p.1 or p.2Full-bleed atmospheric image behind. Warm Aegean architecture / coastline / calm water.
Left: eyebrow label + strong headline + short supporting text + CTA.
Right: three floating glassmorphic circles (Places / Moments / Futures).
Navbar fixed top with blur overlay.
Replace These Texts"THREE WORLDS. ONE CULTURE." → remove entirely
"Experiences that stay with you." → see copy section 16
"Meaningful by design. Unforgettable by nature." → remove entirely
"EXPLORE THE WORLDS" CTA → "Begin here" or "Enter"
MotionCircles oscillate slowly and independently. Hero image: no parallax. Subtle fade-in on page load for text elements (staggered, 200ms delay each).
SECTION 02Brand IntroSCROLL SECTION
LayoutWhite / off-white background. Short text block left or centre. 2–3 sentences max. No image needed here. Let the text breathe. Generous padding.
SECTION 03Two Worlds / Mode PreviewSPLIT SECTION
Layout Reference — PDF p.5Full-width split. Left half: Tourism (light, warm, serif headline, Aegean image). Right half: Entertainment (black, bold sans headline, concert crowd image). Each half has mode name + short description + CTA arrow. This is the strongest homepage section in the mock-ups — use this layout.
Replace Texts"Curated journeys. Timeless places." → see copy section 16
"Live moments. Lasting impact." → see copy section 16
"WHAT CONNECTS CULTURE." → remove
"Kollektiv 26 connects Tourism and Entertainment through culture, design..." → replace with approved brand intro copy
SECTION 04Inquiry CTACLOSING SECTION
LayoutClean, minimal. Off-white background. Short headline centred or left-aligned. Single CTA. Email or link to /contact. No decoration. Let it breathe.

Tourism Page — Layout Direction
Editorial · Premium · Calm
SECTION 01Overview / HeroFULL VIEWPORT
Layout Reference — PDF p.11Left: eyebrow "TOURISM & CONCIERGE" (small caps) + large headline in The Seasons serif + short paragraph + service list below (Destinations / Itineraries / Concierge / Enquire with arrows). Right: full-bleed atmospheric image. Coastline, architecture, water. Stone and warm tones. No gradient overlay needed — image speaks.
Replace Texts"Meaningful travel. Thoughtfully curated." → remove
"From iconic destinations to hidden escapes..." → remove
All existing copy → see copy section 17
SECTION 02Services / AccordionSCROLL SECTION
Layout Reference — PDF p.9Left: section title + short intro paragraph + accordion list (service name + expand icon). Right: full-bleed image, static. Image changes softly when accordion item opens — crossfade to relevant visual. Accordion items: Bespoke Travel / Private Gatherings & Events / Curated Stays / Yachts & Sea / Transfers & Air / Dining & Nightlife / Wellness & Lifestyle.
Replace Texts"BESPOKE ITINERARIES / CURATED STAYS / DESTINATION PLANNING / CONCIERGE ACCESS / LOCAL EXPERTISE" → see copy section 17 for correct service names and descriptions
SECTION 03ItineraryEDITORIAL SECTION
LayoutSingle itinerary example. Not a package grid. Editorial format — day-by-day, prose style. Generous white space. Optional atmospheric image alongside. Content from client — template format ready in copy section 17.
SECTION 04Inquiry CTACLOSING
LayoutClean closing. Short headline + short paragraph + email CTA. → request@kollektiv26.com
12
Entertainment Page — Layout Direction
Contemporary · Energetic · Cultural
SECTION 01Overview / HeroFULL VIEWPORT — BLACK
Layout Reference — PDF p.15Black background. Full or partial concert crowd image (B&W). Left: mode label + large Polymath headline + short paragraph + CTA. Bottom: three section cards (Overview / Focus / Coming Soon) in off-white/dark card strip. Right area: optional yellow event card (placeholder for future events). Waveform/audio marker as decorative system detail — subtle, not dashboard.
Replace These Headlines"ENTERTAINMENT AMPLIFIED." → see copy section 18
"CULTURE IN MOTION." → possible to keep but verify tone
"SOUND. ENERGY. CONNECTED." → replace
"We connect music, culture, and people through live experiences that move." → replace
"Built to move people and shape what's next." → remove
SECTION 02Direction / FocusSCROLL SECTION
LayoutDark background. Section title + short paragraph + 4–6 focus area items. Each item: number + label + one-line description + arrow. Clean numbered list on dark ground. Reference PDF p.14 numbered list structure.
Replace"LIVE EXPERIENCES / SOUND SYSTEMS / CREATIVE DIRECTION" → see copy section 18 for approved focus areas
SECTION 03Coming SoonCLOSING SECTION
LayoutMinimal. Dark background. Short text. Email CTA. entertainment@kollektiv26.com. No decoration. Let honesty be the design.
13
Innovation Page — Layout Direction
Jet black · Yellow glow · Minimal
Layout Reference — PDF p.19Left-aligned layout. Eyebrow "A NEW LAYER OF KOLLEKTIV 26" (small caps). Large "COMING SOON." headline with yellow dot. Short 2-line supporting text. "JOIN THE LIST" CTA with arrow. Right side: yellow (#E8C832) blur glow — large radial, slow pulse animation. Very minimal detail lines. No other content.
FixInnovation glow colour: MUST be #E8C832 (yellow), NOT the orange/amber shown in mock-up p.18. Colour system consistency.
Copy — Final Eyebrow: A NEW LAYER OF KOLLEKTIV 26
Headline: COMING SOON.
Supporting: Something is forming. More when the time is right.
CTA: Join the list →
14
About Page — Layout Direction
Global · Brand foundation · Most text-heavy page
General Neutral, off-white background throughout. Most text-heavy page on the site. Generous line height, generous margins. Typography does the work. Minimal imagery — one strong architectural/atmospheric image maximum. Serif (The Seasons) can appear on pull-quote at large size. Polymath for all body copy.

Section Order

01Intro — brand definition paragraph (from Tunç's revised text)
02The Name / 26 — narrative + pull-quote
03Philosophy — Movement / Gathering / Curation principles
04Current Structure — Tourism (active) / Entertainment (developing) / Innovation (forming)
05Contact close — short sentence + CTA
15
Contact Page — Layout Direction
Global · Simple · Direct
Layout Clean, minimal. Off-white background. No imagery needed.
Short intro headline + paragraph.
Three inquiry type labels: Tourism / Entertainment / General — each with email address.
Optional: simple contact form (name / email / inquiry type / message / send).
Closing note: "Every message is read and answered by the team."

Kollektiv 26 is a contemporary cultural platform shaped by curation, context and perspective. Travel, culture, music and experience are not separated into categories here — they are approached as a continuous field, defined by selection, atmosphere and intention.
Paragraph — TR
Kollektiv 26, küratörlük, bağlam ve kültürel perspektifle şekillenen çağdaş bir kültür platformudur. Seyahat, kültür, müzik ve deneyim burada ayrı kategorilere ayrılmıyor — seçim, atmosfer ve niyet tarafından tanımlanan sürekli bir alan olarak ele alınıyor.

01Overview Hero

Tourism & Concierge
Headline — EN
We don't work from catalogues.

Katalogdan çalışmıyoruz.

02Services Accordion
Section title — EN
What we do
Intro — EN
Our scope covers the full journey — from the first conversation to the last detail. Each area is handled with the same level of consideration, regardless of scale.
01
Bespoke Travel Bespoke Seyahat
ENDestinations, timing and pace — built around how you want to move through the world, not how it's usually sold.
TRDestinasyonlar, zamanlama ve ritim — dünyanın genellikle satıldığı şekilde değil, onu nasıl deneyimlemek istediğinize göre kurgulanır.
02
Private Gatherings & Events Özel Etkinlikler
ENOccasions that don't follow a template. Private parties and intimate gatherings shaped around the people and the place.
TRŞablona uymayan anlar. Özel partiler ve samimi bir araya gelişler — insanlar ve mekan etrafında şekillenir.
03
Curated Stays Villalar & Konaklamalar
ENProperties selected, not listed. Spaces that feel considered rather than catalogued.
TRListelenmiş değil, seçilmiş mekanlar. Katalogdan değil, düşünülerek seçilmiş.
04
Yachts & Sea Yatlar & Deniz
ENYour route, your pace, your water.
TRRotanız, temponuz, suyunuz.
05
Transfers & Air Transfer & Hava
ENHelicopter, private jet and ground — coordinated quietly, without friction.
TRHelikopter, özel jet ve kara — sessizce, sorunsuz koordine edilir.
06
Dining & Nightlife Yemek & Gece
ENThe right table, the right place, the right time. We know where to be.
TRDoğru masa, doğru yer, doğru zaman. Nerede olunacağını biliyoruz.
07
Wellness & Lifestyle
ENRest, recovery and the kind of quiet that has to be arranged. We find it.
TRDinlenme, toparlanma ve ayarlanması gereken türden bir sessizlik. Onu biz buluruz.
03Itinerary Template
Section title — EN
How it can look
Intro — EN
This is not a package. It is a starting point. Every itinerary begins with a conversation and ends differently.
Itinerary content (day-by-day) to be provided by client. Template format: [Title] / [Location — Duration] / Day 01: [activity] / Day 02: [activity] etc. Editorial, prose style. No bullet points.

Line — EN
We respond to every inquiry personally. If something here resonates — or if you're not sure where to start — reach out.
CTA
Begin a conversation → request@kollektiv26.com

02Direction / Focus Areas
Section title — EN
Where we're going
Intro — EN
These are the areas taking shape.
01 Music ProgrammingCurated listening and live experiences. Selection-driven, atmosphere-led.
02 Private GatheringsSound, setting and a considered guest list. Events that don't announce themselves.
03 Cultural ActivationsMoments where music, space and context converge. Site-specific, temporary, intentional.
04 Artist AccessDirect relationships with artists and creative teams. Commissions and first-access programming.
05 After HoursLate. Curated. The kind of night that doesn't have a poster.
06 Venue PartnershipsWorking with spaces that have something to say.

About Copy — Approved
EN + TR
01Intro
Headline — EN
A contemporary cultural system.
Paragraph — EN (Tunç's revised text)
Kollektiv 26 operates as a concierge platform shaped by curation, context and cultural perspective. Rather than separating travel, culture, music and experience into service categories, it approaches them as a continuous field defined by selection, atmosphere and intention. It moves between destinations, disciplines and moments — from the formation of an idea to the experience it becomes. Positioned beyond conventional concierge and hospitality frameworks, Kollektiv 26 is built as a contemporary cultural system.
Headline — TR
Çağdaş bir kültürel sistem.
Paragraph — TR
Kollektiv 26, küratörlük, bağlam ve kültürel perspektifle şekillenen bir concierge platformu olarak faaliyet gösterir. Seyahat, kültür, müzik ve deneyimi ayrı hizmet kategorilerine ayırmak yerine, bunları seçim, atmosfer ve niyet tarafından tanımlanan sürekli bir alan olarak ele alır. Destinasyonlar, disiplinler ve anlar arasında hareket eder — bir fikrin oluşumundan deneyime dönüştüğü ana kadar.
02The Name / 26
Section title
The Name
Paragraph — EN
26 is the number of letters in the Latin alphabet — every story ever told, every direction ever given, every idea ever recorded begins with these marks. A collective is only as strong as the breadth of what it can express. For Kollektiv 26, the name points to language, range and possibility. A platform that can move across different contexts, tones and cultural situations while staying recognisable in how it speaks and presents itself.
Pull-quote
A collective is only as strong as the breadth of what it can express.
03Philosophy
Section title
How we think
Paragraph — EN
The identity is not built around a fixed promise, but around a clear point of view. Every decision — from a destination to a programme — is made through the same lens.
MovementMoving between places, moments and contexts.
GatheringBringing people and cultures into shared spaces.
CurationSelecting over abundance.
04Current Structure
Section title
Where we are now
Intro — EN
Kollektiv 26 is currently active across two disciplines. A third direction is forming.
TourismThe most developed mode. Private travel, concierge access, curated stays and lifestyle coordination across the Aegean and beyond.
EntertainmentAn evolving direction. Music programming, cultural activations and private gatherings. Still forming, but forming with intention.
FutureA third dimension is developing. More when the time is right.
05Contact Close
Line — EN
If something here resonates, we'd like to hear from you.
CTA
Get in touch →
20
Contact Copy — Approved
EN + TR
01Intro
Headline — EN
Have something in mind?
Paragraph — EN
Start here. Whether you're planning a journey, commissioning an experience, or simply want to understand what's possible — this is where it begins. We respond to every inquiry personally.
Headline — TR
Aklınızda bir şey mi var?
Paragraph — TR
Buradan başlayın. Her soruşturma kişisel olarak yanıtlanır.
02Inquiry Types
TourismPrivate travel, concierge coordination, curated stays, lifestyle access. → request@kollektiv26.com
EntertainmentMusic programming, private events, cultural activations, collaborations. → entertainment@kollektiv26.com
GeneralPartnerships, press, introductions. → info@kollektiv26.com
